# <Feature / Product Name> — PRD Draft

> Clarify draft. Items marked `ASSUMPTION` or `OPEN QUESTION` need human
> confirmation. Clarify does not invent business rules.

## Document Profile
<!-- Read by /clarify:finalize (by this heading) to pick the final document standard. -->
- Role: <BA | PO>
- Target standard: <PRD | BRD>   (ASSUMPTION if defaulted by role)
- Domain: <pack name | inferred (no pack — domain items below are labeled)>
- Language: <vi | en | …>   (all output renders in this language; labels
  ASSUMPTION/OPEN QUESTION/SUGGESTION and IDs stay English)

## 0. How to read this draft
### 0.1 What this is
A shaped PRD draft. **Quick read:** skim §1 Summary, §4 "How the system works",
§6 "Requirements", and the open items (§12–§13). Reply to labeled items via the
Answer Sheet (§17). **Diagrams** are kept as source code here; they render visually in
the eventual `prd.html` export.

### 0.2 Symbol conventions
Codes are **stable across versions**; the names beside them are only for reading.

| Symbol | Means | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `F0n-Name` | A functional flow (number is the stable anchor; name is for reading) | `F02-Login` |
| `R#` | A product requirement | `R01` |
| `BR#` | A business rule | `BR3` |
| `A#` / `Q#` / `S#` / `V#` | Assumption / Open Question / Suggestion / Variant | `A1` / `Q2` / `S1` / `V1` |

### 0.3 Glossary
Define each domain term ONCE so the requirements read plainly. Only define terms the
requirements actually use; do not invent terms.

| Term | Plain meaning |
| --- | --- |
| <core term> | <one-line plain meaning> |

## 1. Summary
<One paragraph: the problem, the user, and the intended outcome.>

## 2. Goals & Success Metrics
- Goal: <…>
- Success metric: <measurable target, e.g. +10% activation in 14 days>

## 3. Scope
**In scope**
- <…>

**Out of scope**
- <…>

### 3.1 Variant / Options Matrix (for selection)
When the feature implies multiple product types / channels / configurations,
Clarify proposes candidate options for the user to choose — **not** an assumed
choice. Pick one (or more) by ID in the Answer Sheet (§17).
| ID | Variant | Key differentiators | … |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V1 | <option A> | <…> | <…> |
| V2 | <option B> | <…> | <…> |

## 4. How the system works (overview)
A plain end-to-end narrative so a first-time reader understands the whole before the
parts. Detailed per-flow diagrams live in the flow blocks (`from-spec` / `improve model`).
1. <entry> 2. <choose/see info> 3. <enter details + source/options>
4. <preview> 5. <accept terms> → <authenticate OTP/PIN/biometric>
6. <process> → <result> 7. <view detail / next action>

## 5. Actors / Roles

**User roles**

| Actor | Description | Key permissions |
| --- | --- | --- |
| <…> | <…> | <…> |

**System & external actors** (do not leave empty — list or mark OPEN QUESTION)

| Actor | Type (back-office / system / external / scheduled-job) | Responsibility | Trigger / Schedule |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <e.g. Rate Administrator> | back-office | configures interest rate | on demand |
| <e.g. Interest-Accrual Job> | scheduled-job | accrues daily interest | daily 02:00 |

## 6. Requirements
**One grouped table (Principle 13.7).** Requirements grouped by capability in journey
order; within a group **Must → Should → Could**. Each is a self-standing sentence
(actor + trigger + observable outcome); **Why** is the business reason, not a
cross-reference. Flow / rule links live in §10.1 (Flow Catalog) and the §16 draft
trace register — not inline here; there is no separate traceability **file**
(Principle 13.9). (Leave a blank line before the table — Principle 13.12.)

| ID | Requirement | Why | Priority |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Group A — <capability name>** | | | |
| R01 | When <trigger>, <actor> can <action>, resulting in <outcome>. | <plain business reason> | Must |
| R02 | <…> | <…> | Should |
| **Group B — <capability name>** | | | |
| R03 | <…> | <…> | Must |

## 7. Business Rules
| BR id | Rule (or `OPEN QUESTION`) | Effective from / Version | Applies to (new / existing / both) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| BR1 | <explicit rule> | <date / vN, or n/a> | <new / existing / both> |

Tunable parameters the product exposes (rates, fees, limits, thresholds).
| Parameter | Config store / screen | Owner role | Validation | Effective-dating? | Propagation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <…> | <…> | <…> | <range / format> | <yes/no> | <immediate / on effective date / next job run> |

## 9. Scheduled Jobs / Batch
| Job | Schedule / Trigger | Idempotency / Resume | Inputs | Outputs | Downstream consumers |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <…> | <e.g. daily 02:00 UTC> | <key / resume strategy> | <…> | <…> | <e.g. GL posting, reconciliation> |

## 10. Functional Flows (process-centric)
Flow catalog + screen matrix at draft altitude. Flow IDs are `F0n-Name` (number
stable, name appended). Detailed PlantUML/Mermaid diagrams come from `from-spec` /
`improve model`.

### 10.1 Flow Catalog
The in-document traceability spine: the **Requirement** column must list **every**
`R#` from §6 (no requirement left unmapped; an orphan flow is a finding).

| Flow ID | Business name | Actor(s) | Goal | Related rules (BR) | Requirement (Rxx) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| F01-<Name> | <…> | <…> | <…> | <…> | <R01, R02> |

### 10.2 Screen Information / Display Matrix
Reference each screen's flow as `F0n-Name / step`.
| Screen name | Flow / Step | Purpose | Display fields | User actions | Validation | Error / empty / loading states | UX / content notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <list> | F01-<Name> / 1 | <help user choose> | <options, eligibility, key terms> | <select / filter / compare> | <eligible items only?> | <empty / unavailable / loading> | <plain labels> |
| <confirmation summary> | F01-<Name> / 4 | <review before commitment> | <amount, terms, dates, expected result, source, terms checkbox> | <accept / authenticate / cancel> | <terms accepted, auth required> | <auth unavailable / changed terms> | <no technical jargon> |
| <result> | F01-<Name> / 6 | <show outcome> | <success/failure/pending, reference, next step> | <view detail / retry / contact support> | <n/a> | <unknown / timeout / failed> | <message matches error map> |

## 11. Edge cases, exception handling & messages
The edge analysis lives here in full (no separate matrix file): error-producing edges
in §11.1, non-error edges in §11.3. Groups: boundary, negative/invalid,
exception/failure, illegal state, concurrency, permission, empty/null,
temporal/rule-change, batch/schedule.

### 11.1 Error code & message table
Grouped **by flow** under per-flow anchors (e.g. `{#err-f01}`) so flow steps can
deep-link here. **Step / API** is the business-level step or call (e.g. `step 3` /
`login()`), never a path / method / HTTP status; the entity-state column is dropped
(almost always `unchanged`); user messages avoid technical jargon. The **Test** column
is filled when `from-spec` adds tests.

#### F01-<Name> {#err-f01}

| Code | Step / API | Scenario | Transaction state | User-facing message | Retryable | Required action | Test |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <CODE_001> | step 3 | <…> | <failed / timeout / unknown / reversed> | <plain message> | yes/no | <…> | <T-..> |

### 11.2 State Summary
| Model type | State set | Key transitions / terminal states | Gaps |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Entity state | <e.g. pending, active, closed> | <allowed transitions> | <OPEN QUESTION / none> |
| Transaction / operation state | <initiated, pending-auth, processing, success, failed, timeout, reversed, unknown> | <auth → processing → final state> | <OPEN QUESTION / none> |

### 11.3 Edge cases without errors
Boundary / temporal / concurrency / batch behaviours handled silently or by design
(no user-facing error code): idempotency/replay, TTL/expiry boundaries,
sandbox-vs-production isolation, accepted risks (→ Risks), where relevant.

| Edge | Expected behaviour | Source flow (F0n-Name) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| <e.g. idempotency / replay> | <e.g. duplicate request returns first result, no double effect> | <F0n-Name> |

## 12. Assumptions
In effect unless you override (reply by ID in the Answer Sheet, §17).
- **A1** — ASSUMPTION: <stated default being used>
- **A2** — ASSUMPTION: <…>

Answer by ID in the Answer Sheet (§17). IDs stay stable.

| Item | Impact if unresolved | Owner | Status | Deadline |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Q1** — OPEN QUESTION: <unresolved item blocking confidence> | <what it blocks / risk> | <→ ask: stakeholder> | open | <date> |
| **Q2** — OPEN QUESTION: <…> | <…> | <…> | open | <date> |

## 14. Dependencies, Risks & Non-Functional Requirements
- Dependency / risk: <…>
- NFR: <performance / security / availability / accessibility / compliance>

## 15. Stakeholder Perspectives
Analyze beyond user ↔ system. One row per relevant stakeholder; mark
OPEN QUESTION where the need is unconfirmed.
| Stakeholder | Need / concern from this feature | What they consume / produce |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Operations | <monitoring, manual intervention, support tooling> | <…> |
| Accounting / finance | <GL postings, fees, statements> | <…> |
| Reconciliation | <matching, discrepancy handling> | <…> |
| Partners / external | <integration, SLA, webhooks> | <…> |
| Risk / compliance | <limits, audit, KYC/AML, regulation> | <…> |
| Maintenance / eng | <config, migration, observability, on-call> | <…> |
| Data / analytics | <events, reporting, source of truth> | <…> |
| Security | <authz, sensitive data, retention> | <…> |

## 16. Traceability (draft)
The trace layer for the requirements above: every requirement once (including
deferred — keep the row, mark Status = Deferred), with its **Source** (the
`A#/BR#/S#/Q#` it came from). At `finalize` this is expressed **in-document** via the
Flow Catalog (rule/error/requirement columns) + the Test scenarios table + a Coverage
paragraph — there is no separate traceability-matrix file (Principle 13.9).

| R id | Flow (F0n-Name) | Business rule | Source (← A#/BR#/S#/Q#) | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| R01 | F01-<Name> | BR3 | A1 | Active |

## 17. Suggested Additional Capabilities (for confirmation)
Proactive recommendations to make the product complete — **not yet agreed scope**.
Each is a `SUGGESTION` for you to accept/reject (reply by ID in §18); none is a
confirmed requirement or an invented business rule.
- **S1** — SUGGESTION: <capability (config/admin, reconciliation, reporting,
  notification, audit, migration, serving job …)> — rationale: <why>.
- **S2** — SUGGESTION: <…> — rationale: <…>.

## 18. Answer Sheet (copy, fill in, send back)
Copy this block, fill after each ID, and paste it back — `improve` applies it by ID.
```text
# Variant — pick one id (see §3.1):
Variant: V1
# Assumptions — keep | override: <value>
A1: keep
A2: keep
# Open questions — your answer (or "skip"):
Q1:
Q2:
# Suggestions — yes | no | later:
S1:
S2:
```
